Multiroute pathogen transmission is different
By Samuel V. Scarpino

Social and Interdisciplinary Sciences and Public Health
Research Article  |  SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY
Uncovering simultaneous breakthroughs with a robust measure of disruptiveness
By Munjung Kim, Sadamori Kojaku, et al.
An embedding-based disruption measure not only robustly captures disruptive works but also reveals simultaneous discoveries.
Read more
Research Article  |  EPIDEMIOLOGY
Dynamic shift in the dominant transmission route of clade Ib monkeypox virus across networks with sexual and nonsexual contacts
By Fuminari Miura, Ka Yin Leung, et al.
During an mpox outbreak, the dominant route of transmission and optimal target groups for intervention may shift over time.
Read more
SPONSORED
Economic impact of an urban research university
With eight national centers and over 150 academic programs, Morgan State University is molding the next generation of leaders. Located in the heart of Baltimore, Morgan is finding solutions to urban challenges and driving the local economy.

Neuroscience
Research Article  |  NEUROSCIENCE
Copper deficiency impairs oligodendrocyte maturation and social behavior via mitophagy and mTOR suppression in ASD
By Noriyoshi Usui, Miyuki Doi, et al.
Copper deficiency disrupts brain development and behavior through impaired mitophagy and mTOR signaling.
Read more
Research Article  |  NEUROSCIENCE
Pupil-linked arousal heterogeneously modulates cell-type–specific sensory processing
By Keith J. Kaufman, Rebecca F. Krall, et al.
Two-photon imaging and pupillometry reveal how arousal modulates excitatory neuron subtypes in auditory cortex.
Read more
Research Article  |  OPTICS
Focusing on color: How the eye chooses which wavelength to see best
By Benjamin M. Chin, Martin S. Banks, et al.
Evidence suggests that human vision uses opposing color signals (red-green and blue-yellow) to bring the world into focus.
Read more
Research Article  |  DISEASES AND DISORDERS
Selective vulnerability of cerebral vasculature to NOTCH3 variants in small vessel disease and rescue by phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitor
By Xiangjun Zhao, Chaowen Yu, et al.
iPSC models uncovered disease insights for NOTCH3-SVD, CADASIL, with successful treatment using PDE5 inhibitor sildenafil.

Research Article  |  HUMAN GENETICS
Complex peopling history and expansion events inferred from large-scale modern and ancient Y chromosome sequences
By Guanglin He, Zhiyong Wang, et al.
Ancient Y chromosome lineages trace Paleolithic migrations and Neolithic expansions that shaped East Asia’s paternal history

Research Article  |  GEOCHEMISTRY
Massive wildfires followed oceanic anoxic events during the Late Devonian Frasnian-Famennian mass extinction
By Man Lu, Yongge Sun, et al.
Wildfire was a consequence — not a cause — of widespread marine anoxia during the Late Devonian mass extinction.
Read more

By Zhenyu Luo, Yuhan Soo, et al.
Multiscale trade-linked transportation CO2 emissions reveal carbon responsibility allocation biases and decarbonization potential.
Read more

Abrupt eruptive instability of ice adhered to solid surfaces
By Lei Wang, Kai Wu, et al.
Ice undergoes unexpected eruptive fracture behaviors at low temperatures, driven by thermal stress.

Rising flood risks in semiarid South Asia driven by changing intraseasonal oscillations under global warming
By Jinhui Xie, Pang-Chi Hsu, et al.
Changes in monsoon rhythms are driving more extreme floods in South Asia’s historically dry regions.

The Global Deep Sea Exploration Goals: A representative approach to visually observing the deep seafloor
By Kristen N. Johannes, Susan E. Poulton, et al.
The Global Deep Sea Exploration Goals identify 10,000 locations to create a representative visual dataset of the deep seafloor.

By Yilun Yu, Xing Xu, et al.
Macroevolutionary models show that rib-based breathing released constraints on body size and cranial evolution in early amniotes.

By Chong Sheng, Liuting Yuan, et al.
